subject: Geography
title: Urban Farming
description: A quick win: understand Urban Farming.

hook.text: Singapore grows vegetables in vertical farms stacked 30 stories high inside skyscrapers.

buildup.text: LED lights replace sunlight. Hydroponics replace soil. Yields are 100 times higher per hectare.

discovery.text: Vertical farms use 95% less water than traditional agriculture and zero pesticides.

twist.text: The energy cost is enormous—powering all those lights makes some farms more polluting than fields.

climax.text: If energy goes green, vertical farms could feed megacities without needing farmland at all.

punchline.text: The farm of the future might be on the 30th floor.

quiz.question: How much less water do vertical farms use compared to traditional farming?
